lessonBlocks.instructionId: Latihan ujian sekolah kelas 11: teks eksposisi analitis, sebab-akibat, dan korespondensi formal. Bagian A pilihan ganda, Bagian B menulis.

lessonBlocks.textEn: WHY EVERY STUDENT SHOULD LEARN BASIC BUDGETING. Schools teach us to earn grades, but rarely to manage money — and that gap follows graduates for years. Firstly, budgeting is a daily skill: students already handle allowances, phone credit, and canteen choices, so the classroom examples are free and familiar. Secondly, small financial mistakes compound. A student who never tracks spending becomes an adult surprised by debt; the habit, not the amount, is what matters. Some argue that budgeting is the family's job to teach. Families teach values, certainly — but many parents were never taught the tools themselves, and schools exist precisely to fill such gaps. Therefore, one semester of practical budgeting, using real student cases, should be part of every Grade 11 timetable.

talks.transcriptEn: Good morning, everyone — quick briefing before the doors open. I'll cover three things: zones, breaks, and problems. Zones: your name tag color matches your zone map; stay in your zone unless a coordinator moves you. Breaks: fifteen minutes, staggered — check the schedule behind the info desk, not the group chat, which will be chaos today. Problems: anything involving guests goes to me directly; anything involving equipment goes to the technical booth beside the stage. Do not attempt heroic repairs yourselves — last year someone "fixed" a speaker into permanent silence. That's all. Doors in ten.

lessonBlocks.textEn: NOTICE — TANJUNG LARAS YOUTH GRANT 2027. The city's Creative Youth Grant supports student-led projects with funding of up to 5 million rupiah. What changed this year: applications now require evidence of a completed smaller project — photographs, budgets, or evaluation reports — because last year, roughly half of funded proposals were never carried out. Priority is given to projects that involve more than one school and include a written risk plan. Applications open 1 May and close 30 June; results are announced on 1 August. Note that late submissions and proposals without an adult supervisor's signature will not be reviewed. Questions may be directed to the Youth Affairs Office on Jalan Dermaga.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Wawancara kelas 11 bersama John: pengalaman dan kerja tim. Dinilai dari transkrip dengan rubrik berbicara; tanpa penilaian pelafalan akustik.

johnContent.learningContextId: Wawancara tingkat menengah tentang pengalaman berkelompok dan penanganan masalah.
johnContent.expectedStudentIntent: Menceritakan pengalaman nyata dengan struktur dan menanggapi skenario interaktif.
johnContent.tutorGoal: Menggali contoh spesifik (bukan generalisasi), menguji hedging dan bahasa perbaikan dalam skenario.
johnContent.contextualFollowUpIntent: John menindaklanjuti isi jawaban siswa: jika jawaban umum ("we worked together"), ia minta satu kejadian spesifik dengan siapa/kapan/apa; jika siswa menyalahkan pihak lain, ia bertanya apa yang bisa siswa kendalikan; jika siswa memakai ungkapan target, ia menaikkan kesulitan skenario.

prompts.promptEn: Tell me about a group project you worked on this year. What was your role, and what actually happened?
prompts.followUpIntentId: Minta detail pembagian tugas: siapa mengerjakan apa, dan bagaimana itu diputuskan.

prompts.promptEn: Describe a time when something went wrong in a team task. How did you report it, and what did you do next?
prompts.followUpIntentId: Uji kejujuran laporan: tanyakan kapan masalah dilaporkan dan apakah ada usulan solusi.

prompts.promptEn: Scenario: I'm your teammate, and I insist we finish the whole project tonight, the night before the deadline. Disagree with me — politely.
prompts.followUpIntentId: Balas keberatan siswa dengan argumen tandingan ringan; nilai apakah siswa tetap santun dan menawarkan alternatif.

prompts.promptEn: Scenario continues: we argued about it and I said something harsh. Now I'm upset. Repair the situation.
prompts.followUpIntentId: Nilai tiga bagian perbaikan (akui, tanggung jawab, tindak lanjut); jika siswa hanya minta maaf datar, pancing dengan "sorry for what, exactly?".

prompts.promptEn: Last one: negotiate our study schedule for next week. I'm free evenings only; convince me of a plan that works for both of us.
prompts.followUpIntentId: Tolak usulan pertama dengan alasan realistis satu kali; nilai apakah siswa menawar ulang, bukan menyerah.

criteria.descriptorId: Jawaban menjawab skenario yang diberikan, dengan contoh spesifik.
Bentuk lampau, perfect, dan pengandaian ringan akurat.
Ungkapan kolaborasi/perbaikan kelas 11 digunakan tepat.
Hedging dan pelunak terdengar wajar, tidak dihafal.
Alur kejadian dan solusi mudah diikuti dari transkrip.

lessonBlocks.pronunciationNoteId: Pelafalan TIDAK dinilai: evaluasi akustik belum didukung implementasi (IMPLEMENTATION GAP G3).

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Penilaian presentasi: siswa merekam presentasi 3–4 menit tentang sebuah proyek (nyata atau dari data yang disediakan), lalu menjawab dua pertanyaan lanjutan. Dinilai dari transkrip.

lessonBlocks.taskEn: Present a project of your choice (a real school project, or the provided festival case) in 3–4 minutes. Requirements: a hook, an explicit map ("I'll cover…"), at least two signposted sections, one data description using the provided figures, a "so what" conclusion, and a close.
lessonBlocks.providedDataEn: Festival case figures: visitors 211 (target 150); booth income 1.68 million (target 1.5); complaints 9, of which 6 about queue times; volunteer no-shows 3 of 25.

questions.promptEn: Follow-up 1 (John asks based on the student's actual talk): probe the weakest-supported claim in the presentation and ask for evidence or an example.
questions.followUpIntentId: Adaptif: merujuk klaim nyata dari transkrip siswa.

questions.promptEn: Follow-up 2: "If you ran this project again with half the budget, what would you cut, and why?"

criteria.descriptorId: Hook, peta, bagian berurutan, ringkasan, penutup — semuanya hadir.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: MEETING EXTRACT: A: "Okay, decision time on the charity target. Proposals were the flood victims fund and the school library rebuild." B: "The flood fund is urgent — but honestly, we raised for floods twice last year. Donors are tired." C: "I see that, but the library rebuild is basically invisible. Nobody donates to bookshelves." B: "Unless we make it visible. What if the drama club performs scenes from books the library lost?" A: "…That's actually brilliant. Are we agreed: library rebuild, with the drama tie-in?" ALL: "Agreed." A: "Settled. B, you own the drama club liaison — first update by Friday."

lessonBlocks.textEn: FROM THE CLUB'S FESTIVAL REVIEW (published in the school bulletin): Was the Bridge Festival worth three months of chaos? The numbers say yes: 211 visitors against a target of 150, and booth income twelve percent above plan. The honest answer is more complicated. The festival succeeded because of two decisions made early — phasing down from five schools to two, and writing a real safety plan — and it nearly failed because of one decision we avoided: leaving four task areas with a single owner. That overload led to a broken reporting system, which meant three lost days at the worst possible moment. Some will say the ends justify the means; we disagree. Next year's committee inherits a simple rule written in this year's bruises: no member owns more than two areas, ever. Success that damages people is a loan, not a profit.
